steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Label the side pair for the named angle first: opposite, adjacent, and hypotenuse. Then pick the matching shortcut, such as SOH/CAH/TOA, complementary trig values, or a special right-triangle ratio.

steps.title: Recognize the complementary angle relationship
steps.content: In a right triangle, the two non-right angles must sum to 90 degrees (since all three angles sum to 180 degrees and one is already 90 degrees). The problem confirms $A + B = 90^{\circ}$, so these angles are complementary. When two angles sum to 90 degrees, there's a direct relationship between their sine and cosine:

steps.title: Apply the complementary angle identity
steps.content: Since $A + B = 90^{\circ}$, we have $B = 90^{\circ} - A$. Therefore: $$\sin(A) = \cos(90^{\circ} - A) = \cos(B)$$ We're given that $\sin(A) = \frac{\sqrt{7}}{4}$, so immediately: $$\cos(B) = \frac{\sqrt{7}}{4}$$ That matches the correct answer.
